en-us.html:
"Goo goo g'joob, g'goo goo g'joob"

Story

Go on, move faster, Bash. Faster! My thighs ache as I sprint through the powder, and that's after nearly a full day of marching as well. I hurry onto the breaking ice, which continues to make unsettling cracking noises. I grumble as I skid across the surface of the lake, and as I glance behind me, I see that Treyst has stopped on the shore. There's no way his big metallic beast can set foot on the ice. Nope. That would definitely be a bad idea. Using my hands, I slide rather than walk to the gaping hole that has appeared in the ice. I feel my heart tighten as I scan the oily ripples and unfathomable darkness that lurks beneath. A young woman wearing ice skates explains what happened. Child. Fell. Disappeared... I clench my teeth. I don't need her to spell it out.

Marmo is circling around the hole and waiting for me to help him. I do what he wants and tap on my Construct to envelop him with a shell of heat. I watch him jump in. I close my eyes to look through his senses, and I notice a stream of bubbles rising to the surface. My Alter Ego looks all around, but sees nothing but darkness. We're going to need help... My fingers arrange and rearrange the buttons on the Construct and suddenly a walrus appears next to my dog, complete with a sonar rig and everything it needs to locate the little girl. Using my device, I send it instructions, then watch it dive into the depths. The animal uses its echolocation to probe the icy waters. Hurry, time is running out and every second counts...

Narrator

SUBHASH

blocks.title: Story
paragraphs: Go on, move faster, Bash. Faster! My thighs ache as I sprint through the powder, and that's after nearly a full day of marching as well. I hurry onto the breaking ice, which continues to make unsettling cracking noises. I grumble as I skid across the surface of the lake, and as I glance behind me, I see that Treyst has stopped on the shore. There's no way his big metallic beast can set foot on the ice. Nope. That would definitely be a bad idea. Using my hands, I slide rather than walk to the gaping hole that has appeared in the ice. I feel my heart tighten as I scan the oily ripples and unfathomable darkness that lurks beneath. A young woman wearing ice skates explains what happened. Child. Fell. Disappeared... I clench my teeth. I don't need her to spell it out.

Marmo is circling around the hole and waiting for me to help him. I do what he wants and tap on my Construct to envelop him with a shell of heat. I watch him jump in. I close my eyes to look through his senses, and I notice a stream of bubbles rising to the surface. My Alter Ego looks all around, but sees nothing but darkness. We're going to need help... My fingers arrange and rearrange the buttons on the Construct and suddenly a walrus appears next to my dog, complete with a sonar rig and everything it needs to locate the little girl. Using my device, I send it instructions, then watch it dive into the depths. The animal uses its echolocation to probe the icy waters. Hurry, time is running out and every second counts...

fr-fr.html:
-.-. .. -... .-.. . / . -. / ...- ..- .

Histoire

Allez, plus vite que ça, Bash. Plus vite ! Mes cuisses me font mal tandis que je cours dans la poudreuse, et après presque une journée complète de marche forcée, qui plus est. Je me précipite sur la glace fendillée, qui continue d'émettre des craquements pas très rassurants. Je grommelle en dérapant sur la surface du lac et, en jetant un coup d'œil derrière moi, je vois que Treyst s'est arrêté sur la berge. Pas question que son gros bestiau métallique mette le pied sur la banquise. Non. Ce serait clairement pas une bonne idée. En m'aidant de mes mains, je glisse plus que je marche jusqu'au trou béant qui s'est ouvert dans la glace. Mon cœur se serre tandis que je scrute les ondées huileuses et l'obscurité insondable qui est tapie en dessous. Une jeune femme, chaussée de patins à glace, m'explique ce qui s'est passé. Enfant. Tombée. Disparue... Je serre les dents. J'ai pas besoin qu'on me fasse un dessin.

Marmo, qui tourne autour de l'ouverture, attend que je l'assiste. J'acquiesce et, en pianotant sur mon Construct, l'enveloppe d'une gangue de chaleur. Je le vois plonger. Je ferme les yeux pour voir à travers ses sens, et commence à percevoir un nuage de bulles qui remonte vers la surface. Mon Alter Ego se tourne d'un côté puis de l'autre, mais ne voit que des ténèbres. On va avoir besoin d'aide... Mes doigts combinent et recombinent les touches du Construct et, soudain, un morse jaillit juste à côté de mon chien, équipé d'un sonar et tout ce qu'il faut pour localiser la petite fille. Par le biais de mon appareil, je lui envoie des consignes, et je le vois plonger vers les profondeurs. En usant de son écholocalisation, l'animal sonde les eaux glacées. Allez, le temps presse, chaque seconde compte..

Narré par

SUBHASH

Date

393 AC
